title: Air transport operatives
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate transportation, storage, or distribution activities in accordance with organizational policies and applicable government laws or regulations. Includes logistics managers. Direct or coordinate production, purchasing, warehousing, distribution, or financial forecasting services or activities to limit costs and improve accuracy, customer service, or safety. Examine existing procedures or opportunities for streamlining activities to meet product distribution needs. Direct the movement, storage, or processing of inventory. Perform a variety of attending duties at amusement or recreation facility. May schedule use of recreation facilities, maintain and provide equipment to participants of sporting events or recreational pursuits, or operate amusement concessions and rides. (Based on 16 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Drive trucks with capacities greater than 13 tons, including tractor-trailer combinations, to transport and deliver products, livestock, or other materials. Maneuver trucks into loading or unloading positions, following signals from the loading crew and checking that the vehicle and loading equipment are properly positioned. Load or unload trucks or assist others with loading or unloading, using special loading-related equipment or other necessary equipment. Secure cargo for transport using ropes, blocks, chain, binders, or covers. Report vehicle defects, accidents, traffic violations, or damage to vehicles. Maintain logs of working hours or vehicle service or repair status, following applicable regulations. Check vehicles to ensure mechanical, safety, and emergency equipment is in good working order. Plan or adjust routes based on changing conditions, using navigation devices to minimize fuel consumption and carbon emissions. Collect receipts or signatures for delivered goods and suggest service improvements, as necessary.

Examine existing procedures or opportunities for streamlining activities to meet product distribution needs. Evaluate and select information or technology solutions to improve the tracking and reporting of materials, products distribution, storage, or inventory. Analyze inventories to increase inventory turns, reduce waste, or optimize customer service. Identify or qualify new suppliers in collaboration with other departments, such as procurement, engineering, or quality assurance. Evaluate contractors or business partners for operational efficiency or safety performance records.

Inspect physical conditions of warehouses, vehicle fleets, or equipment and order testing, maintenance, repairs, or replacements. Perform emergency roadside repairs, such as changing tires or installing light bulbs, tire chains, or spark plugs. Develop or implement plans for facility modification or expansion, such as equipment purchases or changes in space allocation or structural design. Conduct inspections to ensure compliance with safety, quality, and service standards.

Prepare accident reports, maintain a safe working environment by monitoring safety procedures and equipment, and train new employees on safety procedures or equipment operation. Oversee recycling pick-up or drop-off programs to comply with community ordinances. Monitor suppliers' activities to assess performance in meeting quality or delivery requirements.

Design or implement supply chains that support environmental policies, and identify opportunities to reuse or recycle materials to minimize consumption of new materials and minimize waste. Review or update supply chain practices in accordance with new or changing environmental policies, standards, regulations, or laws.

Analyze the financial impact of proposed logistics changes and develop plans, policies, or budgets to improve services or increase profits. Negotiate prices and terms with suppliers, vendors, or freight forwarders, and meet with suppliers to discuss performance metrics and changes. Collaborate with other departments to integrate logistics with business systems or processes.

Document physical supply chain processes such as workflows, cycle times, and position responsibilities. Prepare and manage departmental budgets and monitor operations to ensure compliance with administrative policies and safety regulations. Develop procedures for coordinating supply chain management with other functional areas such as sales, marketing, or finance.

Design or implement plans for facility modification or expansion, such as equipment purchases or changes in space allocation or structural design. Negotiate with carriers, warehouse operators, or insurance representatives for services and preferential rates. Analyze all aspects of corporate logistics to determine the most cost-effective means of transporting products or supplies.

In the context of recycling operations, maintain logs of recycling materials received or shipped to processing companies. Supervise recycling technicians and ensure compliance with safety and regulatory standards during recycling operations. Provide training to recycling technicians or community service workers on best practices and safety procedures.

Coordinate shipments of recycling materials with shipping brokers or processing companies and manage shipping records related to recycling or hazardous material services. Inspect physical condition of recycling facilities for compliance with safety, quality, and service standards.

Plan or implement improvements to internal or external systems or processes. Appraise vendor manufacturing capabilities and develop standard cost lists or forecasts for material costs as necessary to enhance logistics performance.

Negotiate contracts with waste management or other firms; implement grant-funded projects, monitoring and reporting progress in accordance with sponsoring agency requirements; develop community recycling plans and goals to minimize waste; oversee campaigns to promote recycling or waste reduction programs; design community solid and hazardous waste management programs.

Determine the quantity and orientation of cargo, compute an aircraft's center of gravity, and verify tank car, barge, or truck load numbers for car placement accuracy; direct ground crews in the loading, unloading, securing, or staging of aircraft cargo or baggage; distribute cargo to maximize use of space, and check specifications of materials loaded or unloaded against information contained in work orders.

Operate industrial trucks, tractors, loaders, and equipment to transport materials to and from loading docks and warehouses; monitor product movement to and from storage tanks, coordinating activities with other workers; start pumps and adjust valves to regulate the flow of products; sort materials into appropriate containers for recycling.

Inspect equipment for wear and conformance to specifications; clean, inspect, or lubricate recyclable collection equipment; perform routine maintenance or minor repairs on recycling equipment; and resolve personnel problems or complaints.
Plan work schedules, assign duties to staff for effective performance of activities, and assess training needs of staff to provide appropriate instruction.
Prepare and maintain work records and reports of employee time, wages, sales, or inspections; transmit and explain work orders to laborers; and record logs of recycled materials or waste chemicals.

Conduct staff meetings to relay general information; evaluate employee performance and prepare performance appraisals; and recommend or implement personnel actions such as employee selection, evaluation, or rewards.
Ensure adherence to safety procedures and rules; monitor activities and take action if safety violations occur; and comply with cleaning, inspection, or maintenance standards in work areas.
Collaborate with workers and managers to solve work-related problems; confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or resolve issues.

Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments; check conditions and weights of vessels to ensure compliance with loading procedures; and inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, or equipment for safety and specification compliance.

Sort cargo before loading and unloading; observe positions of cars passing loading spouts and adjust spouts for accurate loading; and prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities and operations.

Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials; review work throughout the work process to ensure it is performed properly; and oversee daily operational activities to ensure targets are met.

Conduct inspections of job sites to assess maintenance or repair needs; gather relevant data to estimate resource requirements for projects, and recommend measures to improve efficiency and customer services.

Provide information about facilities, entertainment options and rules and regulations, and maintain inventories of equipment by storing, retrieving, assembling, and disassembling items as necessary; monitor safety procedures and perform risk assessments.

Evaluate and implement measures to improve worker motivation or workflows; resolve worker problems and collaborate with employees for effective solutions; and review production schedules or other notices to determine shipping dates and requirements.

Inspect materials or facilities to ensure conformance to safety or operational standards; implement corrective actions when deficiencies are identified; and maintain cleanliness of equipment storage areas and workspaces.

Review credentials, conduct interviews, or make hiring recommendations; participate in ongoing training and education to stay updated on industry trends; and implement comprehensive employee training in operational procedures and functions.
